{"step_by_step_thinking": "Tamsulosin, Sildenafil, and Prazosin are all alpha-blockers, which primarily relax the muscles in the prostate and bladder neck to improve urine flow. They do not reduce the size of the prostate. Finasteride, on the other hand, is a 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor that blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone, which leads to a reduction in prostate size over time. The documents consistently mention that Finasteride and Dutasteride are effective in reducing prostate size.", "answer_choice": "C"}
